title: The GeoFront in EoE
wikitext:
[]: The GeoFront in EoE.             Quote Post by Replicator » Mon Dec 31, 2012 4:04 pm When the Black Moon emerges from underground, and is ejected into space, how did NERV HQ still remain standing, when the GeoFront is practically the Black Moon. According to the map, the GeoFront (NERV HQ) only occupies a very small upper level of the Black Moon, approximately 0.9 km in height, out of a total diameter of 13.75 km for the total GeoFront. If the HQ was only on the tip of the GeoFront, then how were Fuyutsuki and co. able to watch the Black Moon float to space, when they were still in the control room? GeoFront diagram SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 31, 2012 4:10 pm Observation posts scattered around the Hakone region, as they used to track angels, I would imagine.

[]:             Quote Post by Replicator » Mon Dec 31, 2012 4:12 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Observation posts scattered around the Hakone region, as they used to track angels, I would imagine.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Mon Dec 31, 2012 4:33 pm IIRC, Nerv HQ was moved to a different location after Zeruel and Unit-01 trashed it. However, by the time EoE happens they seem to be back in the original HQ, since we can see Ritsuko modifying the MAGI's self-destruct protocol, and we know the MAGI weren't moved to the secondary base. We also see the "lid" of the Geofront being blown off by an N2 mine, as well as the Nerv personnel clearly feeling the shockwave from it. So we know they were still in the HQ while all of this was going on. Do we ever actually see them watching the Black Moon rise up off of the Earth? If so, Fuyutsuki and the Bridge Bunnies were probably watching through some kind of satellite feed. I'm wondering how they were even alive at that point, given the lack of oxygen in outer space. GNR hax, maybe?

[]:             Quote Post by Atropos » Mon Dec 31, 2012 5:08 pm riffraff11235 wrote: I'm wondering how they were even alive at that point, given the lack of oxygen in outer space. GNR hax, maybe?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Dec 31, 2012 5:26 pm riffraff11235 wrote: I'm wondering how they were even alive at that point,

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Dec 31, 2012 5:32 pm riffraff11235 wrote: IIRC, Nerv HQ was moved to a different location after Zeruel and Unit-01 trashed it.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Jan 07, 2013 6:53 pm Which, of course, results in a continuity error for EoE, seeing as everybody is back in Nerv HQ.

[]:             Quote Post by fizz450_03 » Wed Feb 20, 2013 1:30 am i'm under the impression that what was shown as the 2nd command center and the one that got mushed should look pretty much the same? ritsuko did say that while the stuff there is old, the lay out is the same as the main command center...

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Wed Feb 20, 2013 8:57 am fizz450_03 wrote: i'm under the impression that what was shown as the 2nd command center and the one that got mushed should look pretty much the same? ritsuko did say that while the stuff there is old, the lay out is the same as the main command center...

[]:             Quote Post by Replicator » Wed Feb 20, 2013 8:58 am I don't know, but there's an even bigger problem. Is the GeoFront large enough that its gravitational field would be sufficient to prevent all of the people inside from floating into zero gravity? You don't see the Bridge Crew and the equipment flying everywhere when the Black Moon bursts out of the ground. Unless NERV had some sort of plan for this all along, that would include possibly magnetic devices powerful enough to generate artificial gravity? But, its also possible that there is some sort of axial rotation when the Black Moon emerges from the Earth, as you can see cyclonic around its perimeter. Any physics buffs in here?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Feb 20, 2013 9:12 am Physics is exactly what you don't need - it would spoil everything!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Feb 20, 2013 11:00 am Replicator wrote: Is the GeoFront large enough that its gravitational field would be sufficient to prevent all of the people inside from floating into zero gravity?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Feb 20, 2013 11:33 am Given the Black Moon's size, it's clear from the rate at which it rose that it was moving incredibly fast, so everyone inside should have been liquefied. Oh, wait.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Feb 20, 2013 11:34 am It's somewhere deep inside of a plot hole.

[]:             Quote Post by fizz450_03 » Wed Feb 20, 2013 8:00 pm Shinoyami65 wrote: Yeah, but it's never directly addressed in EoE which command centre they're in . My two cents is that they are on the Black Moon and have enough oxygen to survive the brief time they spend in outer space; during the ascent the PA makes a comment on the alititude and entering "F layer" which I think is meant to refer to the alititude of the base, indicating that it's rising with the Black Moon.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Wed Feb 20, 2013 10:58 pm Chuckman wrote: Given the Black Moon's size, it's clear from the rate at which it rose that it was moving incredibly fast, so everyone inside should have been liquefied.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Feb 20, 2013 11:16 pm I was making a tang joke, but it looked to me like it went a few miles in about a few seconds or so, which is pretty fast. I'm pretty sure everyone was goo by the point it really got going but I would imagine lifting from underground to the upper atmosphere in like ten seconds would shove everyone through the floor. It's hard to judge how fast it was actually going, since we're looking at three really huge objects (GNR, Earth, and the Black Moon) with a really screwed up sense of scale. Also it's a cartoon. The speed of the Geofront's movement is nothing compared to the absurdity of GNR and the rest of it, though. I wouldn't worry too much about the actual mechanics.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Feb 21, 2013 5:13 am Between instrumentality weirdness and shot editing it's kind of impossible to know exactly how much time is passing in most of that movie, and therefore how fast things are happening. It could have taken an hour or more to reach it's max elevation but Anno certainly isn't going to waste screen time showing that. For example, how many days have passed between the opening Shinji fails at drowning, the hospital scene, and the start of the attack? How long does the attack itself last from the first guard getting knifed in the back to crossplosionpocalypse? With day/night cycling happening at least once in there somewhere it's rather hard to be sure. If those aren't clear, good luck puzzling out how long Shinji was in the LCL sea or how long he was alone afterwards.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Thu Feb 21, 2013 7:43 am Thanks to AT-field magic, the movement of geofront happens in a series of millimeter space-time warps which means it doesn't really "move" instead of just staying still while the fabric of space-time is getting torn to shreds around it. There, solved it for you.

According to the map, the GeoFront (NERV HQ) only occupies a very small upper level of the Black Moon, approximately 0.9 km in height, out of a total diameter of 13.75 km for the total G
